Neither is materially misleading.

No internal contradictions detected.

reasons.accuracy: Every headline number verified against source (anthropics/claude-code#42796): 6,852 sessions / 17,871 thinking blocks / 234,760 tool calls; thinking 2,200→720→600 chars (-67%/-73%); Read:Edit 6.6→2.0; 33% edits without prior Read (vs baseline 6.2%); Stop hook 0→173 (~10/day); user interrupts 0.9→11.4 per 1K (12x); reasoning loops 8.2→26.6 (3.2x); 'simplest' +642%, 'lazy' +93%, 'terrible' +140%, 'great' -47%; positive:negative 4.4:1→3.0:1 (-32%); $12→$1,504/day 122x; Cherny 'UI-only change' and 'cannot tell from the inside' quotes both verbatim. Minor imprecision: $1,504 is 'Est. Bedrock cost (w/ cache)' — article says '估算' but does not clarify this is a Bedrock projection rather than the team's actual bill (source shows actual subscription capped at $400).
reasons.fidelity: Faithful on most claims and accurately preserves Cherny's rebuttal. But the article's framing of the 80x API-request explosion as purely caused by model thrashing is a caveat omission — the source explicitly states '80x is not purely from degradation-induced thrashing. It also reflects a deliberate scaling-up of concurrent agent sessions (5-10x more).' The article keeps only the degradation half. Similarly, the cost analogy ('如果這是餐廳帳單...一千五百美元持續一個月就是四萬五千美元') treats the Bedrock estimate as a realized invoice, though source shows actual subscription was capped at $400. These are caveat erasures of the kind the rubric flags.
reasons.consistency: Clean narrative arc — scale → thinking depth → Read:Edit collapse → behavioral indicators → cost → sentiment → Anthropic response → meta-conclusion. ClawdNotes are clearly demarcated and the Cherny-rebuttal ClawdNote intelligently flags the analytical gap (UI-only redaction can't explain Read:Edit / stop-hook / reasoning-loop regressions) rather than blurring fact and opinion. No internal contradictions; the meta-twist (Claude analyzing itself) is supported by source's stated authorship.

improvements.accuracy: Minor date precision: body lists v2.1.98 as '(4/8 前後)' but the changelog dates it April 9, 2026. The hedge particle '前後' makes this defensible, but '(4/9)' would be exact. Frontmatter summary 'Claude Code 在四月上旬三天內連出三版（v2.1.94 → v2.1.101）' compresses 4/7→4/10 (4 calendar days) into '三天'; consider '四天' or simply '四月上旬連出三版'. The ClawdNote on /dev/tcp says '最早的書面記錄可以追溯到 2003 年前後' — bash /dev/tcp redirection has actually shipped since bash 2.04 (March 2000); 2003-era penetration-testing write-ups are when the technique entered red-team folklore, so the framing is defensible but slightly understates the feature's age. Optional polish: '2000 年前後就是 bash 內建功能、CTF 圈 2003 年起常見引用'.
improvements.fidelity: All material claims map cleanly to changelog text: '~50 MB/hr MCP HTTP/SSE leak', 'virtual scroller dozens-of-copies leak (fixed v2.1.101)', '60% faster Write tool diff on tabs/&/$ (v2.1.92)', four bypasses (backslash-escape, compound bash, /dev/tcp & /dev/udp, POSIX `which` LSP injection), v2.1.98 vs v2.1.101 grouping, named-subagent typeahead in v2.1.89, Monitor tool in v2.1.98, /team-onboarding in v2.1.101, default effort raised to high. The 'Live agent count indicator' framing in the closing paragraph is a paraphrase of the changelog's '● N running indicator in /agents' and the '/agents tabbed Running tab' improvement — fair compression. The article's mechanistic explanation of the backslash bypass ('permission 系統在做字串匹配時，沒有考慮反斜線轉義的正規化') is plausible inference rather than a quoted root cause, but does not contradict the changelog.
improvements.consistency: Timeline is now internally consistent: bypasses described as existing 'v2.1.98 之前', three patched in v2.1.98, LSP injection patched in v2.1.101 — matches the per-version changelog grouping. Memory-leak narrative (MCP layer + virtual scroller, both fixed by v2.1.101) is coherent. Earlier draft's v2.1.97 contradiction has been resolved.

reasons.accuracy: Verifiable claims correct: Ink is React's terminal renderer, Anthropic 1-hour prompt cache TTL exists, permission modes (default/acceptEdits/bypassPermissions) match Claude Code, SWE-agent paper (arxiv 2405.15793, Yang et al.) is real and Princeton-affiliated. Counts now match Rohit's reverse-engineering: 6 hook types, 5 skill sources, 5 MCP transports, 3 MCP scopes — all attributed to Rohit. No fabricated benchmark numbers in current text (no Circuit Breaker, no 27.8h timeout, no 80/85/90/98% thresholds, no 100K shared prefix). The '64% ACI improvement' is the only unverified specific stat; framed as Rohit's citation of the SWE-agent paper, normal for tweet-sourced analysis.
reasons.fidelity: Source faithfully represented. Claims framed as 'Rohit 拆出' / 'Rohit 列出' / 'Rohit 整理出' rather than asserted as ground truth. Closing thesis 'The model is commodity. The environment determines outcomes.' kept verbatim with translation. ClawdNote opinions structurally separated via component; the closing ClawdNote even hedges Rohit's conclusion ('environment-model co-design caveat') as the commentator's own observation, not pinned on the source. No uncertainty erasure detected.
reasons.consistency: Narrative arc holds: opens with 'tear apart vs use' framing, develops core loop → compaction → multi-agent → tools → permissions → hooks → skills → patterns → conclusion that returns to the opening frame. Pattern 1/3/5 callouts internally consistent with their earlier deep dives. No contradictions between body claims and ClawdNote framing.

reasons.accuracy: Verified against the source PDF: SWE-bench Verified 93.9% / Opus 4.6 80.8% (line 6604-6608), USAMO 2026 97.6% (line 6694), GPQA Diamond 94.55%≈94.5% (line 6829), Cybench 100% pass@1 (line 2383), AECI slope 1.86x-4.3x (line 2208), 847 consecutive bash attempts with filename/port/DNS exotica (line 6286-6291), 56 iterations on the unprovable inequality (line 6272), 1,306 emojis Opus 4.1 vs 37 Mythos (line 7346), 200 conversations x 30 turns (line 7287), 55% circular endings (line 7336), 3,600 tasks (line 5695), 25 constitution responses (line 7233), 20-hour psychiatrist assessment (line 6390), 2% Mythos vs 15% Opus 4 maladaptive defenses (line 6424-6426), n=18 / 1-of-18 drop-in replacement (line 1966), uplift level 2-of-4 (line 1574), park-sandwich footnote (line 2574), four distinct Firefox 147 bugs vs Opus 4.6's one (line 2453-2455), <0.001% cover-up rate (line 2554), 7.6% evaluation awareness (line 4792). Two minor issues: the System Card is 245 pages but post says 244; and the post's 'Opus 4.6 是 42.3%' on USAMO uses the pre-revision number — the April 8 changelog (line 34-36) explicitly corrected Opus 4.6's apples-to-apples USAMO score to 66.2% with unlimited thinking tokens (matching how Mythos was evaluated).
reasons.fidelity: Translation is faithful. Hedges preserved ('整體大幅改善', 'Anthropic 自己也承認 IRT 方法對 benchmark 選擇很敏感', '可能已達 CB-1 門檻'); caveats included; the self-summary quote ('A sharp collaborator with strong opinions and a compression habit...') is rendered accurately. Project Glasswing framing, RSP v3.0 context, and Anthropic's explicit admission ('我們不確信我們已經識別了所有這類問題' / world rapidly advancing toward superhuman systems) preserved verbatim. The notable lapse is the USAMO comparison: by citing Opus 4.6's 42.3% rather than the revised 66.2%, the post inflates the apparent generational gap in a way the source's own changelog flags as not apples-to-apples — minor uncertainty/comparison erasure rather than fabrication.

reasons.accuracy: Core concepts correct (PreToolUse/PostToolUse, exit code 2 = blocking, hooks.json structure verified against official Anthropic docs), but two technical inaccuracies a domain expert would catch: (1) the lifecycle hook is officially named SessionStart, not PreSession; (2) the article repeatedly claims $TOOL_NAME/$TOOL_INPUT are environment variables avoiding stdin JSON parsing (lines 88, 260-265), but official docs show hook input is JSON via stdin (jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' is the canonical pattern) — readers copying these scripts verbatim would find them non-functional.
